title: cajanus volubilis
intro: Cajanus volubilis, also known as the Climbing Pigeonwings or Bengal gram, is a type of flowering plant native to South Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and skin disorders, and has been shown to have anti-cancer and anti-diabetic properties. Cajanus volubilis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, and is also used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine practices.
